contradictions.claim: Customer concentration: Two customers represented 77% of revenue.

contradicting_evidence.evidence: Customer A (likely Microsoft) was 71% of Q2 2025 revenue, with the next three customers each <10%.

contradictions.nature_of_conflict: Press release states top two customers = 77% of revenue without period; analyst report shows 71% for top customer alone in Q2 2025, implying top two likely exceed 77%, creating temporal and compositional inconsistency.

contradictions.claim: Revenue in most recent year was $1.9 billion with 700% YoY growth.

contradicting_evidence.evidence: Reported $1.2 billion revenue in Q2 2025, suggesting annualized run-rate far exceeds $1.9B, conflicting with press release's implied annual figure.

contradictions.nature_of_conflict: Press release implies $1.9B is latest annual revenue with 700% growth; analyst report shows $1.2B in a single quarter (Q2 2025), indicating either misalignment on reporting period or growth magnitude.

contradictions.claim: Total debt as of June 30, 2025 was $11.17 billion.

contradicting_evidence.evidence: As of December 31, 2024, total debt principal was $8.03 billion; no disclosure of $11.17B as of June 30, 2025 exists in filing.

contradictions.nature_of_conflict: Analyst report cites $11.17B total debt as of June 30, 2025; S-1 only discloses $8.03B as of year-end 2024 and does not confirm the higher mid-2025 figure.

contradictions.claim: The company has industry-leading performance due to being purpose-built for AI workloads.
contradictions.nature_of_conflict: Promotional claim about architectural superiority remains unverified by independent technical benchmarks or third-party validation in the packet.

contradictions.claim: CoreWeave is the AI Hyperscaler driving the AI revolution.
contradictions.nature_of_conflict: Management-style promotional assertion without corroborating market share data, independent analyst consensus, or regulatory endorsement.

verified_claims.claim: Two customers represented 77% of revenue for the year ended December 31, 2024.
Microsoft accounted for 62% of revenue for the year ended December 31, 2024.
Revenue for the year ended December 31, 2024 was approximately $1.9 billion with 737% YoY growth.
Operating loss for the year ended December 31, 2024 was approximately $863 million.
Cash balance as of December 31, 2024 was approximately $1.36 billion.
Company disclosed material weaknesses in internal control over financial reporting.
